{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Bishop's Opening: Ponziani-Urusov Gambit| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | |nd| | |= | | | | |pd| | | |= | | |bl|pl|pl| | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl| |nl|rl|= || }} == Bishop's Opening: Ponziani-Urusov Gambit == === 3. d4!? === The Ponziani-Urusov Gambit is a line in which White sacrifices a pawn early in the game. White will occupy the center at the beginning of the game and challenge Black’s central control. '''Black's strongest and most natural response is to accept the gambit with [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Bc4/2...Nf6/3. d4/3...exd4|3...exd4]].''' If Black tries to transpose to the [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...Nc6/3. Bc4/3...Nf6/4. d4/4...exd4|Two Knights Defence]] with 3...Nc6?!, then White will have 4. d5!, which prevents the transposition and will cause Black trouble. == Theory table == {{ChessTable}}. '''1. e4 e5 2. Bc4 Nf6 3. d4!?'''
